exemplar.title: To what extent has the use of User-Generated Content (UGC) on TikTok impacted e.l.f.'s brand positioning and customer retention in terms of market share and branding?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the use of User-Generated Content (UGC) on TikTok impacted e.l.f.'s brand positioning and customer retention in terms of market share and branding?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and coherent communication of the topic throughout introduction and headings
Adequate range of secondary sources with recognition of limitations
weaknesses: Research question remains somewhat broad with dual focus
Methodology lacks defined analytical framework or tools
Justification for specific source selection needs deeper explanation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place two elements solidly in the 3â4 band and one at the lower end of that band, indicating an overall performance consistent with a mark of 4 out of 6. The topic and question are communicated adequately, but greater focus and methodological rigor are required to reach the top band.

strengths: Generally relevant application of around twenty varied sources
Clear and coherent subject knowledge of marketing concepts
Mostly accurate use of key terminology
weaknesses: Numerous unreferenced statements and uncited images
Inconsistent brand name formatting and occasional conceptual confusion
Minor spelling and precision errors in terminology
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fit the mid-band (3â4) yet lack the consistency or depth for the top band. The work demonstrates clear knowledge but only partially effective source integration and terminology precision, meriting 3 out of 6.

strengths: Fair variety of relevant secondary research sources
Clear analytical elements (SWOT, positioning map) with supported conclusions
Logical argument development in discussion and conclusion
weaknesses: Absence of primary data limits research depth
Analysis often descriptive with causal links asserted rather than tested
Evaluation is brief and seldom critically reflective
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands align with the 4â6 band descriptors. While the student demonstrates good research and clear analysis, the work stops short of critical depth. Awarding the top of the 4â6 band (6/12) aligns with strand assessments and maintains proportionality in the overall 17-mark allocation.

strengths: Meets EE structural requirements (title page, contents, chapters, bibliography)
Logical organization of sections and clear headings
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation style and superscript usage
Missing page numbers in the main text and some typographical errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure falls in the highest band (3â4) and layout in the mid-band (1â2). The overall presentation performance best matches the 3â4 band descriptor, justifying the maximum 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly worded, focused on the extent to which UGC on TikTok affects brand positioning and customer retention at e.l.f.  It is clearly linked to the ensuing discussion; however, its dual focus (brand positioning and customer retention) and two different performance indicators make it only partially narrow.  Thus it satisfies the âclearly stated but only partially focusedâ description of the 3-4 band.

The discussion is logical and the conclusion summarises benefits and limitations, but evaluation is brief and seldom critical (e.g., no consideration of financial metrics vs. social metrics, or methodological shortcomings).  Arguments are developed but only partially critical, aligning with the 4-6 descriptor; a conservative score of 4 is appropriate.

The essay shows clear knowledge of marketing concepts (7Ps, USP, guerrilla marketing, market orientation) and links them to the case.  However, coverage is descriptive and occasionally inaccurate (confusing social-marketing definition, spelling of âguerrillaâ).  Sources are not always critically used.  This fits the mid-band âknowledge is clear but sources only partially effectively appliedâ.

Terminology such as âUGCâ, âmarket shareâ, âbrand positioningâ and âstakeholdersâ is used regularly and mostly correctly.  Minor errors (âquerrillaâ, âe. I. fâ) and lapses in precision prevent consistent accuracy, so the strand merits the 3-4 band with a score of 3.

The student interprets graphs, provides a SWOT and positioning map, and links evidence to claims (e.g., sales spike tied to UGC).  Yet many arguments remain descriptive and causal links are asserted rather than rigorously tested; alternative explanations are rarely explored.  This constitutes âclear analysis with well-supported conclusionsâ at the lower end of the 4-6 band (score 4).

Around twenty sources are cited and several statistics/figures are incorporated, so relevance is generally sound.  Nevertheless, many statements remain unreferenced, some images lack captions, and data are often accepted at face value.  This demonstrates âmostly relevant and appropriateâ but not consistently well-integrated application; hence a middle-band mark of 3.

The methodology section lists secondary sources (company site, market reports, TikTok metrics) and recognises their limitations.  No primary data or explicit analytical tools are planned.  Source selection is partly justified but range is moderate and largely web-based, showing âsome evidence of informed selectionâ rather than clear evidence of effectiveness.  This aligns with the lower half of the 3-4 band (score 3).

Research draws on a fair variety of trade articles, statistics (Statista, Yahoo Finance) and academic texts; most are directly tied to the question.  The data set is not extensive and lacks primary evidence, but relevance to the RQ is clear, placing the work in the âgoodâ 4-6 band.  A mid-band score of 5 reflects adequate breadth without excellence.

Basic EE formatting elements are present (word count, headings, some figure captions), but inconsistent citation style, irregular superscript notation, missing page numbers in the main text, and several typographical errors weaken overall layout accuracy.  This corresponds to âsome layout considerations missing or incorrectâ with a score of 2.

The student states a clear topic â the impact of TikTok-based UGC on e.l.f.âs brand positioning and retention â and reiterates it consistently through the introduction and section headings.  Key business constructs to be examined (market share, branding, engagement) are briefly outlined, giving sufficientânot comprehensiveâcontext.  While the explanation lacks depth about why this topic is important in the wider discipline, the communication is generally coherent and complete, matching the âadequateâ descriptor of the 3-4 band.

The essay contains a title page, abstract-style introduction, contents page, logical chapter structure, figures, conclusion, and bibliographyâmeeting EE structural requirements.  Therefore it fits the âclearly appropriate structureâ descriptor at the top band (3-4) with a score of 4.

annotations.comment: The investigation title is prominently displayed and clearly defines the research focus.
The word count is provided and remains within the 4 000-word limit, demonstrating adherence to Extended Essay formatting requirements.
The table of contents lists major sections clearly, but consider adding sub-level headings for key subsections to improve navigation.
The heading âTable of contentsâ uses inconsistent capitalization; apply a uniform style throughout to enhance professional presentation.
The dual focus of the research question on both brand positioning and customer retention makes it somewhat broad; consider narrowing to one primary performance indicator for sharper analysis.
The brand name is inconsistently formatted as âe. I. fâ; ensure consistent spelling and capitalization (e.l.f.) to maintain clarity and correct terminology.
The methodology section explains data sources clearly but lacks a framework for analysis; consider specifying analytical tools or models to guide interpretation.
Discussion of secondary source limitations is insightful, but the justification for choosing specific market reports requires deeper explanation of their reliability and relevance.
The phrasing âback-up in business theoriesâ is informal; revise to âsupported by business theoriesâ for academic precision.
The early history narrative contains inconsistent brand name formatting (âe.I.fâ); ensure e.l.f. is used consistently for professional accuracy.
Figure 1âs caption lists the source inline; consider adopting a consistent caption format (figure number, title, source, date) to align with EE guidelines.
The distinction between social media marketing and social marketing is unclear; refine the definitions with precise examples to avoid conceptual confusion.
Footnotes display inconsistent superscript coding (e.g., â<sup>&</sup>â); standardize footnote format to maintain a professional academic presentation.
The ethical branding discussion is descriptive; consider analysing how ethical values directly impact customer retention metrics to deepen evaluation.
Multiple footnotes on this page appear misnumbered and overlap; ensure sequential and non-overlapping citations for clarity.
The Instagram follower figures are presented visually, but there is no integrated discussion in the text; link these data directly to your analysis of engagement.
The platform name âTik Tokâ is split in the caption; correct to the single word âTikTokâ to maintain proper terminology.
The guerrilla marketing strategy is misspelt as âguerillaâ; revise to the standard spelling âguerrilla marketingâ for accuracy.
Footnote style here includes HTML entities; convert these to plain text superscripts and full citations to improve readability.
The TikTok algorithm discussion cites 61% user perception; integrate a brief critique of this dataâs limitations rather than presenting it uncritically.
The algorithm description is well written but would benefit from referencing the Nielsen study directly in-text to reinforce the evidence base.
The metrics cited for the Eyes, Lips, Face challenge require clearer in-text citation (superscript placement) to link each statistic to its source.
Footnote numbers on this page do not align with the in-text superscripts; verify numbering consistency to avoid reader confusion.
The Beauty Squad section cites membership figures but does not evaluate their impact on retention; add analysis of how these tiers influenced loyalty metrics.
Graph 1 caption lists author and year but omits the data source formatting; include âSource: Canva, AbadÃ­a, 2024â in a standard caption style.
The interpretation of the positioning map remains descriptive; deepen analysis by discussing strategic implications for e.l.f. relative to each competitor.
